result:
queue: point-to-point, one consumer wins each message, work distribution, load balancing across workers
topic: pub/sub, every subscriber gets own copy, fan-out, event broadcasting

pick queue when: task must run exactly once (send email, process payment, resize image), workers are interchangeable, you want backpressure via consumer count
pick topic when: multiple independent systems react to same event (order placed → billing + inventory + analytics + email all need it), subscribers evolve independently, you don't know all consumers up front

modern brokers blur line: kafka "topics" act queue-like via consumer groups (group = queue semantics, multiple groups = topic semantics). rabbitmq queues bind to exchanges for fan-out. so real question: do consumers compete for messages or each get copy?

rule of thumb: command → queue, event → topic
